assertions.text: Vehicle was traveling east at about 85 km/h before drifting off the road.
Driver applied the brakes (stood on the brakes) well before leaving the roadway.
Braking occurred before the vehicle left the roadway.
Vehicle drifted off the road (departed the roadway) while traveling east at about 85 km/h.
The front of the car struck the bridge abutment.
There was at least one impact—the front of the car striking the bridge abutment.
The front of the car struck the bridge abutment (implying a collision with fixed structure); severity not explicitly quantified.

verdicts.evidence: The speed samples show the vehicle traveling between [77.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s and 90.0 km/h at t=-0.5 s](T2), with the EDR pre-impact first/last values reported as [first_value 77.0 and last_value 87.0](T3); the claimed [about 85 km/h] is outside the recorded range at the stated time and not supported (record supports ~77–90 km/h, last recorded [87.0 km/h](T3)).

The brake channel shows all samples as off with no transitions over the pre-crash window: [brake values all 0.0 at times -5.0 to 0.0](T2) and compute_stats reports [fraction_on 0.0 with no transitions](T3), contradicting the claim that the driver 'stood on the brakes.'

Because the brake channel remained off throughout the pre-crash period ([all brake samples 0.0 from -5.0 to 0.0](T2) and [fraction_on 0.0](T3)), there is no telemetry evidence of braking before departure; thus the claim that braking occurred before leaving the roadway is contradicted by the sensors.

The speed during the pre-crash window is shown as [77.0–90.0 km/h with last_value 87.0 km/h](T2)(T3), but telemetry has no channel that directly records 'departing the roadway' or heading 'east' vs west; GPS heading or lane-departure channels are not present, so the assertion that the vehicle 'drifted off the road while traveling east at about 85 km/h' is unverifiable (speed portion is measured [first/last 77.0/87.0 km/h](T3) but heading/departure are not available).

The crash pulse shows a large negative longitudinal delta-V of [−87.0 km/h occurring at t=0.09 s](T3), consistent with a frontal impact (vehicle decelerated rapidly), supporting the statement that the front of the car struck a fixed structure such as a bridge abutment.

The EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded 1](T1) and the crash-pulse delta-V trace shows a single major longitudinal change (final cumulative delta-V [−87.0 km/h](T3)), supporting that there was at least one impact.

The sensors record a large crash pulse with longitudinal delta-V max [−87.101 km/h at 90 ms](T1) indicating a high-severity collision with a fixed structure; severity is not explicitly quantified in the narrative, and telemetry shows the large delta-V consistent with a substantial impact.

result.rationale: Pre-crash telemetry contradicts the claimed braking and the precise claimed speed; impact telemetry supports a high-severity frontal impact and that at least one impact occurred. Some claims about roadway departure and heading are unverifiable due to lack of GPS/heading channels.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
